sections.paragraph:
to leap, to skip, Ecc 3:4. Figuratively, the phrase is used to skip for fear (compare Hiph. נָתַר Piel), Psa 114:4, Psa 114:6. (Syr., Pa., id. The primary idea appears to be that of trampling the ground with one’s feet, see רָקַק ).

Piel, to spring, to dance, 1Ch 15:29 Isa 13:21 Job 21:11 used figuratively, of a chariot driven rapidly over rough ways, Nah 3:2 Joe 2:5.

Hiphil, prop. to cause to leap, i.e. to tremble, used of the mountains, Psa 29:6. Comp. Kal, also רָעַשׁ and נָתַר.
